scope: G group round 2 match predictions with betting-risk gates

risk_gate.red_team_policy: no steady-banker language when revise/hold gates remain
risk_gate.special_guard: G/H round-1 Belgium miss correction is active

model_update: Egypt counterattack and draw path up
New Zealand open-play and set-piece tail up

prediction.lean: Belgium slight win lean, draw protection required

market_snapshot.type: public odds snippet, not full same-book live snapshot
market_snapshot.market_note: Model is materially cooler on Belgium because of round-1 favorite-conversion failure, red-team revise gate, and Iran scoring paths.

china_sports_lottery_notes: Belgium win is a slight first direction, not a steady banker.
If handicap is Belgium -1, avoid relying on handicap win.
invalidate_if: Belgium rotates or minutes-limits De Bruyne, Lukaku or Doku
Iran core attackers are fit and Belgium keeps a high defensive line
Belgium price shortens further without lineup support

prediction.lean: Egypt win lean, draw protection high

market_snapshot.type: public odds snippet, not full same-book live snapshot
market_snapshot.market_note: Model is cooler on Egypt because Salah/Marmoush status is not locked and New Zealand's attacking tail rose after the 2-2 draw with Iran.

china_sports_lottery_notes: Egypt win is the first direction but not a steady banker.
If handicap is Egypt -1, avoid relying on handicap win.
Main total-goal band is 1/2 with 3 as tail cover.
invalidate_if: Salah or Marmoush are not fully available
Chris Wood starts at full fitness and New Zealand set-piece setup is strong
Egypt price shortens sharply without lineup support

aggregate_notes: G group round 2 keeps a higher draw baseline because all four round-1 teams drew.
Belgium market pricing is much hotter than the model, so Belgium cannot be treated as a betting banker.
Egypt is a first-direction favorite against New Zealand, but both Egypt and New Zealand red-team gates remain hold until core player status is confirmed.
